---
pagetitle: "Research"
---

My main research interests are causal inference and time-to-event analysis. In particular, I have worked on methods for estimating the effects of time-varying treatments on survival outcomes under time-dependent confounding, and multistate modelling of event history data. I also have an interest in methods for infectious diseases, such as in studies of vaccine effects, excess mortality estimation, infectious disease surveillance and infectious disease modelling.

I am interested in applications to both randomised and observational studies, including linked clinical and population wide registry data. E.g., I am currently involved in projects on sick leave and work-related outcomes with researchers at [the National Institute of Occupational Health in Norway](https://stami.no/en/) and on drug use and patient outcomes after traumatic injury with researchers at [the Oslo University Hospital](https://www.ous-research.no/ipot/).

For publications see my [Google Scholar profile](https://scholar.google.no/citations?hl=en&pli=1&user=GRG21B8AAAAJ).
